description: Living glossary lifecycle: merge, decay, promote, prune rules for wiki/glossary/user-query-patterns.md

action.description: Find existing entry with same matched_process + similar intent
Merge: usage_count++, last_used=current_date, append new examples to list
Remove duplicate entry — keep only merged version

merge_rules.constraint: Never create duplicate entries — always merge first

prune_action.description: Move stale entries to 'Stale' section at end of file
If no new usage within next 30 days → remove from file entirely

thresholds.description: Pattern promoted to 'High-Confidence Patterns' section at top
Add note: 'agent auto-routes without heuristic check — pattern is reliable'

write_contract.rule: Clear & Rewrite — never append to glossary file

steps.description: modify in memory: merge/decay/promote as needed

write_contract.constraint: Max 1 glossary write per session — batch all updates into single write

read_contract.rule: Grep-only access — never read full file in context

allowed.purpose: lookup similar patterns during intent detection
read high-confidence section only (top of file)

prohibited.reason: file grows over time — token overload risk
no -m flag — could output many lines

query_step_0.5.description: After query_intent_decoder heuristic match → grep glossary for confidence boost

lint_check_id=16.description: Glossary decay/cleanup check — run at end of lint session
lint_check_id=16.action: read glossary → mark stale entries → write back if changes needed

session_end.description: Batch all pattern updates into single glossary write

non_blocking_rule.rule: Glossary operations are non-blocking — if grep fails or write skipped, continue process flow
non_blocking_rule.fallback: If glossary lookup returns no results → rely on hardcoded heuristics only
